So I'm going to do mine right now because I think it'd be fun. And if I bomb, it doesn't matter. It's not selling anything. So who cares? Right? That's the nice thing. It's like, I messed up. It doesn't matter. You're not selling anything yet. All right. So I'm going to do send you to yes. I'm going to Facebook Live, live video. I'm going to call this one my ClickFunnel story. Story. Click the dot. I'm just going to call that. I'm just going to do story time. My Facebook Live is the most interactional time. It's me in my backyard with the crickets telling a story. Me and my kids, it's talking. It's really fun and interesting. So it doesn't always have to be a sales pitch. Stuff like this will build community better than almost anything else you can do. So I wrote out in the book my Pifty Bridge story. And as I was going through this, I realized that I was only telling half the story. Because anyway, so it's kind of cool. So I have mine all around here. So you want to see like, when I did the exercise, it's all in there inside the book. You can go through all the questions and kind of see how it all relates. And then how it kind of relates back to this. So what's going to happen is when we do the webinars tonight, the perfect webinar to start going through it, what happens is typically someone got on the webinar. And the first thing I'm going to do is I'm going to introduce the big domino. And I'm going to tell my Pifty Bridge story. And the goal of that Pifty Bridge story is knock the domino down. And sometimes it is. That's all it takes. And then they're ready to buy. And other times it doesn't. And that's when we get into all the content of the webinar, the three secrets. We're trying to do anything that's holding that domino up. We're trying to knock it out. But that's kind of the concept. So this is the story, the Pifty Bridge, excuse me, the origin story is what I'm typically telling in the first of my presentation. And that's kind of the context of where this is going to fit. As you'll see more later on today. So, all right. We need to get a high top chair someday so I can sit in those things for stuff like this. But I'm just going to sit down here. And hopefully I ended up going off the wire fire yesterday, right? To make it work. All right, LTE. Yeah, do we have, oh heck yeah. But John's going to have my story stool. I do now. I didn't before. Welcome to the story stool. Yeah. Dave's got, oh I'm so excited. The stool story. Thank you very much. All right, cool. The story stool. Hey guys, the story stool. The story stool. Well, this story stool is autographed by a storyteller. Just kidding. All right. Okay, I've never told a story live in all the details. I'm going to go through and hopefully won't mess up. I've got my notes. I'm sure I will. Okay, here we go. And we're going live in three, two, one. Hey everyone, this is Russell. Hope you guys are all doing awesome. So I'm here right now in the room of I think 48 or 49 amazing entrepreneurs. And you guys all see me again. And we've been talking about storytelling and like how to tell your story. Kind of the origin story of where you got started and what got you into what you're doing. And so I've been challenging all of them to go and do that. And I thought it would be kind of cool if I just told you guys. My story and some of you guys have heard pieces or parts of this, but probably not the whole thing. And so what I'm going to do is just kind of share with you guys a story about about click funnels and why this for me became my new opportunity. The thing that I'm so excited about and why I have so much passion. Some people say Russell, you've been talking about click funnels every day for the last two and a half years. I'm like, I know, because I'm so excited every day. So some of the backstory if you don't know. So I got started in this business, I guess it's almost like 13 or 14 years ago now. And when I did, I was a college guys going to Boise State University, which is just down the road from it right now. And I was like a wrestler, which means I wrestled and I had to go to school. So I barely graduated. It was like barely like, I think my accumulative was a 2.1, which means I got a whole bunch of C's like one B over like five years. So it's kind of painful. But but I graduated, which made my mom happy, which is pretty cool. But during school, as I was wrestling, I started learning about this internet marketing stuff. And you have to remember this is 12 years ago, pre, you know, it's before Facebook, it's before Instagram, all these kind of things like we had Google kind of. And in fact, I don't remember how we were driving traffic. We didn't have website, anything like I was using front page. Some of the old timers remember front page. So I was creating these things in front page and it was it was pretty bad. So I was doing that. But I believe that it was it was possible. I saw people like some of my mentors were Armand Moran and Alex Mendoza and some of these guys who were making money online. I was like, God, these guys can do it. Like, I'm pretty sure I can do it. So that was my goal I wanted to accomplish. Now, the other side of that I don't really share a lot of times was it wasn't just that I wanted to make money. That was a desire I had. But like the bigger desires I had just gotten married to my beautiful wife. We're still married now. And I was 15 years this year, which is amazing. And and I married her. She was working full time to support me, her jobless wrestling husband. And I don't know about you, but like I was a vision like when I got married, I'd be like the man and I would be the man. And I would be like the supporter. And it was it was honestly pretty tough for me that she was supporting me and I didn't have anything to contribute other than like, I'm going to go to school and wrestle and hopefully. And it was hard for me. And I remember like, I got to figure out a way to contribute. I don't want her doing everything. And it was it was. And so I was like the real driving factor. Like if I can, in fact, my goal is if I make a thousand dollars a month, that would like match what she was making. We were making very much money at the time. I was like, then like, I feel like I'm contributing as well. And so that was my goal. Like, I need to figure out how to do this. And I tried all sorts of stuff. I was doing, I was trying eBay. I was trying Craigslist. I was doing stuff. And we had like little like, I had like a $20 month, which was like big deal for me. I was excited, but like none of the things really hit big. And about the time I started learning about information marketing. In fact, I was at a post office mailing something I bought from eBay in this huge awkward box. I thought it was going to sell huge and I ended up selling like a buck 50 or something. I lost tons of money. And then I did a postage and it was a nightmare. And I'm sitting in the line at the post office next to this dude who had a big box full of CDs, like thousands of them. I'm like, dude, what are you selling? He's like, well, I have one CD I sell it and I burn it. And I'm like, so these are orders for like the month. He's like, no, these are my orders for this week. I'm like, I'm doing the math. How much you selling for is like 67 bucks. I was like, oh my gosh. And I had this like, this like, I need to sell information like that is the future. So I started trying to figure out information. I started doing stuff. My very first product I ever created was a DVD on how to make potato guns. I sure some of you guys have, have heard this story before. So I started making potato guns and it was working really good. But then what I was doing is I was going to Google, I was buying ads on Google and we were making some money back and forth. But eventually I probably had four or five months of success and then Google changed their algorithm and it got really, really hard. And the harder thing for me was like during this time, like it was me building things in front page and I had to figure out how to like FTP pages and images and then how to go to PayPal and get the order button. It was like, it was super tech and I'm not a techie person at all. But after Google changed our algorithm, we tried some stuff and I remember one of my friends called me, Mike Philson, some of you guys may know Mike and he said, he said, Russell, I figured out like how to make this whole internet marketing game work again. I'm like, what do you mean? Is your potato gun thing still making money? I was like, no, like I can't, I can't break even I can't make a profit. He's like, well, I went back to all my sites and I added these things and he called them OTOs. I was like, what's an OTO? He's like, it's an upsell and one time offer. Oh, so he's like, I had these upsells and people start buying the upsells. And suddenly like I'm making two or three times as much money from everything I sold and I was like, dude, I can actually do that. And so I remember jumping online. I was Googling like potato guns and trying to figure out like what would my upsell be? I don't know. All I had was a potato gun DVD and I met this dude up in Northern Idaho who actually made potato guns and dropped shipped him. And I was like, dude, if I sell those, will you drop shipping for me? He was like, yeah, like I love to not have to sell these things. And so I partnered with him and I started selling DVDs and people started buying this upsell. And it was the first thing I had made a funnel and I was like, this is a funnel like, and that was like this new thing. And I was so excited. And so I had this epiphany like this is the future. Like if I can make more funnels like this one's going to make me like, I don't know, 20, 30 bucks a day. But what if I had two funnels or three or five or 10? And so I started going a little bit crazy and I created funnels in the couponing market, which by the way is a horrible market. People in that market do not like to spend money. So I don't do that one. But I did couponing. We did dating. We did weight loss. We did network marketing. I've done diabetic supplements. We've done and that's like the ones on top of my head. Like there's like, I show you guys, there's like a trail of thousands of offers. Some of the work and a lot more that didn't work. But we were doing these things over and over in my plans. Like I had 10 of them that we should make in a hundred bucks a day or 20 or 50 or whatever it is. So I was doing this and at first it was working. But like the problem I kept running into was like from pages for something like me was hard and I had like to do these pages. And I looked ugly. Other people's like look awesome. So I had a designer and he would design these things. So I'd make it in front pages ugly. I sent him. I looked cool and I put it up. Then it was up and then I couldn't do anything. I couldn't touch it again. Like I had one shot and then I had like bug him. So then I hired like a web guy to go and like do edits for me. And then like I was just using PayPal and I couldn't do anything else. And so then someone like invented like a one click up sell. So I was like, dude, I need that. So I had to hire a programmer to connect that to the next thing. And like it kept getting more and more complicated to the point where like soon I wasn't able to do anything. I just look at it and be like, change that. And then they try to change it and they didn't. I'm like, no, they move it over here and then they put it in the wrong spot. It would take days, sometimes weeks going back and forth with the guys in Romania and India and everywhere trying to get them to just move an image and make it a little bit bigger. Like things that like were so common sense, but I couldn't actually do it. It drove me nuts. And about that time, one of my friends and partners, in fact, he may not be in the room right now, is Todd in here still? Oh, he's already in lunch. So he's already gone. But Todd was like one of my partners that were doing these things with me and he saw my frustration. He's like, what if we built something where like, and the joke was like that Russell could actually like edit the website and actually could move things. He quit bugging us all the time. And I was like, dude, that'd be awesome. He's like, okay, that's what we're going to create. And that day we sat in front of a white board show and mapped it. I'm like, what would it look like? How would it work? And so we mapped out this vision of what became ClickFunnels and he went home and actually built it. And then it was funny him. And then another part we brought in was Dylan Jones. They were working. He did the editor and they would build part of it. And they're like, we need to make this so simple. Russell can do it. So they do it and they say, Russell, test this. And I log in and I'm like, how do I do it? He's like, we're not going to tell you. I'm like, okay, so I'd like do some stuff. And if I could figure it out, then like, good, we got it. If I got stuck, I'm like, I don't know what to do. Then like, okay, we didn't do it intuitively enough. So I went back and forth and back and forth for probably six or seven months. And finally it was a point where I got it. I was like, if I can do this, like then anyone can. And first it was just a tool for me. Like that was really the initial goal of ClickFunnels. Like I think it was a ton down to like this way Russell will quit bugging us. And I don't have to design sites anymore or redo his headers. Like all this stuff that was happening. And then after I started using it and I was having success, I was like, we have to make this a tool forever. And I get free all entrepreneurs and like it freed me. And so because of that, I was able to start creating funnels quickly. I went from having five or six guys and take us on average almost two to three months every single time to build a funnel. Where I was actually able to do it by myself in like an hour and hour and a half sometimes sometimes less, which was cool. And so the cool thing is like I was able to achieve that and have success. We started rolling out funnels like crazy. Some of you guys probably remember seeing a lot of them. I still do a lot of them because it's so much fun to do. But the cooler thing for me was the other side. Like I was able to make money to support my wife and support my kids because I didn't no longer had to spend three months doing something. And my time to be with my kids is completely freed up. Like I have a chance to like go home every day and play with my kids. I'm not like stressing out trying to like talk to programmers in India at three in the morning and like stressing all the stuff. Like it just just works. And that was really the biggest thing for me. And it's been an amazing thing. In fact, now my kids are using clickfunnels. They're building stuff and clickfunnels. And it's like not just, you know, not just for me, it's not just a product. It's a mission. I'm seeing people's lives change. I'm seeing other people who had messages, but they couldn't get them out because they couldn't move things around on this stupid page. We're now like the power is back in their hand. And that's what that's what fires me up is being able to help entrepreneurs and that kind of thing. So that's kind of my clickfunnels story. So if you guys haven't heard it before, I want to share that with you guys and to share why I'm so passionate about this. And you know, for me, it's not just, I talk about this all the time, but like it's not a matter if you're going to become a clickfunnels member. It's when because it's the only way to do what we're talking about. Like the only alternative is to do what I just used to do 10 years ago and hire guys in Romania and India and the Philippines. I think have a team of 30 people to be able to move an image or you can just use clickfunnels and it gives you and me, the entrepreneurs, the power back, which is awesome. So I hope that helps you guys and hope you guys see why I'm so passionate about clickfunnels and with that said, I'm going to check out. And if you like this comment down below. If you have any friends who are like, what is that clickfunnel thing? Share this to them tag down below so they can see why me why I'm so passionate and probably why you're so passionate too.
